context: Evaluates correct use of AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption with crypto-browserify. The solution must use createCipheriv with 'aes-256-gcm', call getAuthTag() after encryption, and use setAuthTag() before calling final() during decryption.

checklist.name: Uses aes-256-gcm algorithm
checklist.description: The solution passes 'aes-256-gcm' as the algorithm to createCipheriv and createDecipheriv.

checklist.name: Calls getAuthTag()
checklist.description: After calling cipher.final() during encryption, the solution calls cipher.getAuthTag() to retrieve the authentication tag.

checklist.name: Calls setAuthTag()
checklist.description: Before calling decipher.final() during decryption, the solution calls decipher.setAuthTag(authTag) to set the tag for verification.

checklist.name: Auth tag encoding
checklist.description: The auth tag is correctly encoded (e.g., as hex) when returned from encryptGCM and correctly decoded when passed to setAuthTag in decryptGCM.

checklist.name: Integrity verification throws
checklist.description: When decryptGCM is called with a tampered auth tag or ciphertext, the call to decipher.final() throws an error (the GCM tag mismatch propagates).
